description: API Deployment Workflow - Create GitHub Actions workflow for deploying the Azure Functions API and update MANUAL_SETUP_NOTES.md with instructions for setting up the publish profile secret
steps: 1. Create .github/workflows/deploy-api.yml: Trigger on push to main when api/** files change. Jobs: build_and_deploy running on ubuntu-latest. Steps: checkout, setup Node.js 22, setup pnpm v10, pnpm install --frozen-lockfile in api/, pnpm build in api/, deploy using Azure/functions-action@v1 with app-name from env var and publish-profile from secrets.AZURE_FUNCTIONAPP_PUBLISH_PROFILE. Follow the same pattern as deploy-frontend.yml for consistency.

2. Update docs/MANUAL_SETUP_NOTES.md: Add a new section 'I. API Deployment via GitHub Actions' between sections H and the end. Include: how to get the publish profile from Azure Portal (Azure Functions > your function app > Get publish profile), how to add it as GitHub secret AZURE_FUNCTIONAPP_PUBLISH_PROFILE (Settings > Secrets and variables > Actions > New repository secret), note that the API reads CSV files from api/src/data/ which are generated by running the ML notebooks (notebooks 01-08), and the workflow triggers automatically on push to main when api/ files change.
acceptance_criteria: .github/workflows/deploy-api.yml exists with correct trigger, build, and deploy steps
docs/MANUAL_SETUP_NOTES.md has a section explaining how to get and configure the publish profile
The workflow YAML is valid syntax
verification: Run pnpm build in frontend/ to ensure nothing is broken (this feature only adds workflow and docs)
findings: Workflow YAML validated. Frontend build passes with all 16 routes. PyYAML parses 'on:' as True (boolean) which is standard behavior for GitHub Actions YAML files - GitHub handles this correctly.
